question_text: What happens to a spring if you exceed its elastic limit?

options.label: B
options.text: It becomes permanently deformed and doesn't return to its original shape

correct_answer: It becomes permanently deformed and doesn't return to its original shape
explanation: The elastic limit is the maximum force a spring can withstand while still returning to its original shape when the force is removed. If you exceed this limit, the spring undergoes plastic deformation, meaning it is permanently stretched or bent and will not spring back to its original position.
